notes: Game doesn't work with DX11, it require patched wine, but even with it you will have graphical glitches, so stick with DX9ex. Shattering and freezing, unexpected disconnections from the server also affects Windows user, so it's not wine fault.
title: Path of Exile

notes:
Firstly I started a game with this launch options: PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%
I had few issues during menus or game itself (shuttering, slowdowns, game quits on menus, exceptions during entering areas). All graphic settings set to lowest possible (or disable if possible), DX9 (game doesn't run on DX11, exception appears on game start).
Then I used this launch options (taken from comments here): PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% --waitforpreload --nologo --nodx9ex --gc2
Bingo, no more issues, any! Game runs flawlessly. I'm recommending this launch options (all graphic settings still set to lowest possible or disabled at all, DX9 used).
title: Path of Exile

notes:
I got really good performance on dx11 with this https://github.com/kakra/wine/releases and turning vsync off.
I had to run 1080p though or it would crash.

This is how I installed it. In steam->settings->Steam Play use Proton 3.16-6 Beta.
Launch the game and it should install that proton if you don't have it.
Go to where your steam games are installed and find steamapps/common/Proton 3.16 Beta.
Replace the dist-folder with the dist from the "hack" you downloaded from the github link.

Run Path of exile and make sure dx11 is selected. If you want the old proton back just delete it and revalidate the files from steam.
title: Path of Exile

notes:
Dont use vsync and multithreading in ingame options. Then you are left with some minor stuttering when shaders are loaded.
To get rid of it just use DXVK_STATE_CACHE=0 in Steam's launch options.

And DX11 works out of the box now. So good graphics without stuttering.

notes: To begin with, getting the game to start and generate the config file required me to have my monitor set to 1080p. DX11 works, but produces stutters (that are not FPS drops but something else), and only runs at 1080p. DX9 with DXVK_STATE_CACHE=0 and multi-threading disabled is the best solution for me, providing smooth gameplay. Unfortunately, running at any resolution other than 1080p will work fine until you use a portal when the game will crash. All other suggested launch options were tested and didn't produce any notable results.

notes: Beware of playing on resolutions higher than 1920x1080. When trying to start/play the game in 2560x1440 resolution, the game would black screen and freeze (but the main menu music would play) at the Grinding Gear Games logo. Setting my monitor resolution to 1920x1080 and saving video settings to windowed or full screen mode at 1920x1080 would allow the game to start and run; even after closing the game. After setting monitor resolution to 1920x1080 to get the game running you can switch your monitor back to it's native resolution (2560x1440 in this case). Don't try to change the game resolution to any higher as it will crash your game and possibly your system.

notes: Worked flawlessly after setting the resolution to 1920x1080. I have 2560x1440 on desktop and as one reviewer pointed out the screen gets black after the video. The trick was to switch desktop res. to 1920x1080, start the game and set 1920x1080 res in options then quit and set desktop resolution back to 2560x1440.
title: Path of Exile

notes: After disabling Esync, runs perfectly fine with no tweaks to settings. Game runs on high / Ultra settings at solid 60 FPS with the exception of towns and heavily populated areas, at which point there are very occasional hiccups. Completely playable.
title: Path of Exile

notes:
Runs perfectly. Had to install the 32-bit vulkan icd loader package to get it running though (game seems to use both 32 and 64 bit libraries).
I recommend running with the options %command% --waitforpreload --gc 2 --nologo --noasync.
In particular, the --waitforpreload helps to build a shader cache before the game and areas load. This helps reduce stuttering and low FPS.
title: Path of Exile

notes: First I got a black screen and the game froze after the loading gears at startup. Then I set my screen resolution to 1920x1080 and now the game loaded and was playable. I then exited and set my native 1440p resolution and the game now work as intended. Also using the following launch options: %command% --waitforpreload --gc 2 --nologo --noasync

title: Path of Exile

notes:
Game works okay out of the box. Found on reddit that using: DXVK_STATE_CACHE=0 will help performance a bit. It stutters as it loads new stuff, but it quickly disappears. After a few hours of playing, it barely stutters at all and keep a constant framerate at all time, sometimes even surpassing the performance I get on Windows. 

The only thing for me preventing a platinum rating are the stutters that you will encouter at first. They do go away overtime, so it is fine for me right now, but not everybody might like it. 

Some people have had more luck running the standalone version on Lutris (here's a guide: (https://www.reddit.com/r/pathofexile/comments/9x47or/guide_getting_path_of_exiledx11_to_run_smoothly/)

notes:
The first launch did without any settings. Launched, the menu is working fine. then I connected to the server, and when the download is complete, at the moment when the loading screen should disappear, I get a Wine error, and it immediately closes and does not have time to do anything.

Then I tried to play with tweaks and ONLY played more stable with 'Disable Esync'. But there is a problem with switching between windows (alt + tab: default), but not always.

Perhaps this is somehow related to the KDE Plasma and window animation. There were also stutters, but after a while they became much smaller. The only thing that has not yet been tested completely is the familiar guys from the betrayal. The first meeting with them was soaked with lags, and when I tried to turn to June, the game immediately crashed. There were several departures when changing locations, but in general it works no worse than the windows. Is that with a slight drawdown of FPS with a large amount of what is happening on the screen. although on the windows I had the same thing, but a little bit less.

P. S. sorry for my english, i hope google translator could understand me))

notes:
I've played Path of Exile on Linux for about a month. I used to run the game with just PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% --nologo and had performance comparable to Windows with occasional fps drops when loading new assets.

title: Path of Exile

notes: Upon start, it displays numerous 'exception' errors/messages and then terminates. I read that Proton doesn't support DX11, so I first attempted to edit the production_Config.ini and change from 11 to 9, but had the same exceptions. So I disabled d3d11.dll via Launch Options, but same issues. Then I read that PoE has discontinued the use of DX9. I also attempted to run in Proton 3.16-4, 3.16-9 (Beta), and 3.7-8 with no luck.

title: Path of Exile

notes:
After testing, it really does work with ESync disabled. You can do this with this string in the Launch Options: "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%" Looking through the reports, the main issues come from people who use Integrated Graphics/iGPU's, including mobile GPU's. It could also be the fact that it's Intel, since they don't have specific drivers for Linux AFAIK. If you're having freezing or pausing on AMD, disable Engine Multithreading and it will fix it. That's an issue with the game itself, though, not the emulation.

Point is, yes, it works, and you can thank Intel/iGPU's if you're having issues since they have shoddy drivers.

notes:
4.2-7 UPDATE: Runs, but lost performance from 4.2-6; memory leaks are now much more commonplace, with stuttering now a common issue after moderate to long amounts of gameplay. It's fixed by restarting the game client. After further testing, you are most likely to run into issues if you are running integrated graphics, an iGPU, or a mobile GPU. Otherwise, it's playable.


Current Launch Options string for gameplay: "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% --nologo --waitforpreload --gc 2"

notes: Performance is way better than previous versions of Proton. Loading objects in does not lag as bad as it used to. Using a monolith will sometimes freeze the game for a split second, but has not crashed on me. A few graphical glitches here and there like a monster will appear a solid red color. Performance is almost native windows. One problem I am having is server disconnecting issues.
title: Path of Exile

notes:
Just got done reaching lvl 40 on a Scion, with the new PoE update (3.7.1) that fixed the crashing surrounding Legion mobs, and the new Proton update (4.2-7), it runs perfectly. You don't even need Proton arguments anymore; you can run it out of the box. Most issues that you could face (load times, freezes, and other hiccups) are actual issues with the game itself, as tested on Win7 and Win10 (gag).

Point is, provided that they don't screw up something with the memory or further updates, Path of Exile has officially been completed outside of mobile GPU/GC incompatibility. You can play this with no tweaks and no emulation issues at all.

notes: HI guys! To continue with my report. I reached tier 9 maps at the atlas. The game runs flawlessly with no tweaks at all, however you may encounter some heavy stutter specially on boss encounter as your system needs to cache all of the animations and assets, this may be very dangerous to any build, so i did some tinkering for a bit and remembered the tweaks we used to apply to the steam running options when proton didn't existed. So i tried the "waitforpreload" string in launch options, this option basically does what it says it waits until all of the assets for a given map are preloaded, and Bam! the game now runs perfectly with absolutely no stuttering at all, i have to mention that back then when i first tried this option i didn't have an SSD and the downside was that i took a decent amount of time to load each new instance or map, but with and SSD the load time is almost as fast as without the option (i would say about 2 or 3 seconds more load time). Hope this help you all Exiles and you enjoy this amazing game with the power of freedom powered by GNU/Linux.

notes:
Installation ran without issues, initially stuttering was experienced.

Sometimes errors like "CreateResourceViewEx" appear, but the game loads. I recommand you to restart the game. Last time the error occurred, the map at the waypoints screen was missing.

Used a custom command line: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% --nologo --waitforpreload --gc 2 --noasync 

--waitforpreload optimized loading times

Note: Got PoEMacro working, this is how:

1) Download AutoHotKey 1.1 Installer from https://www.autohotkey.com/download/1.1/
2) Move the Installer to your C drive, on my system located here: ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/238960/pfx/drive_c/
3) Login to PoE with your Character and open the Options (fastest way)
4) Select UI and scroll to the bottom, click on "Show Folder"
5) An explorer will appear. Navigate to the Installer and install it
6) Download PoeMacro from here: https://github.com/PoE-TradeMacro/POE-TradeMacro and place it in your C: drive
7) Open the explorer the same way again and double-click the PoEMacro ahk Script
8) Now you should be able to use all hotkeys in-game

Note: Sometimes the price predictions will appear and disappear instantly. In that case it helps to switch to windowed mode. I currently don't have a clue what could make the popup be persistent in fullscreen, if someone knows how - tell :)

Desktop environment is KDE.

title: Path of Exile

notes: Almost flawless at this point, looks and runs better than native and improving daily, shader caching seems to be responsible for all client crashes/spikes, the longer you play the better the performance. Just did 8 hour session of ripper maps in parties crashless with win users complaining about lag, now giving gold rating.  After a few hundred hours of "testing" this works best for me at 1080p ~40fps: default settings, global illumination, predictive networking, vsync off, MSAA x4, trilinear filtering, launch options --waitforpreload --gc 2 . At lowest settings and resolution fps is the same. Restart game after any adjustments and steam after shader downloads.
title: Path of Exile

notes:
I started With the the game without any runtime options at all.  Game loads just fine, and even plays.  However, as soon as I entered the "Tidal Coast" to complete the first mission "Mercy Mission"; my entire desktop would freeze.  I would be able to hear the waves crashing on the shore.  But the monitor would go completely black and i can't even hit CTRL+ALT+DEL; I must hold down the power button on my machine.  My heart broke for my SSD which PoE is installed on.

So round two i used the runtime options "--noasync --gc2 --waitforpreload", the game took longer to load the the main menu; but seem to run better in game.  Still the exact same situation in the Tidal Coast.  Have to hold my power button down and risk destroying my SSD.  I did have to run a fsck after this shut down.  Booted into emergency mode!

So round three!  I removed the previous set of runtime options, and used only then "PROTON_USE_WINED3D" option.  SUCCESS!!!!!   i can actually load the Tidal Coast map now!!!

There is another however; after about 10-15 minutes of gameplay; My second monitor will stop showing my desktop background, and turn completely gray.  If i try exit the application (Either by hitting quit to OS, or quit to Character Select); My machine still locks up and i must hold down the power button.

I have LLVM7, Python 3.7.4, vulkan-icd-load & lib32-vulkan-icd-loader, Nvidia proprietary drivers installed.   I dont know what else to try.  But i think i may just wait so i dont risk damaging my machine any further

title: Path of Exile

notes:
As a disclaimer, I haven't put any effort into optimizing anything beyond in-game settings.

Overall, I'd say the game is playable and everything works. No crashes.  The major issue is performance, particularly when the effects and projectiles start flying which isn't all that surprising since even on windows this is also an issue.  But I'm experiencing about 50-60% less fps across the board vs same system on Windows 10.

In my opinion the game is 'playable' if you're playing solo and are willing to take more care once you're character starts to reach end-game when all the effects are getting insane and avoid hardcore unless you're a special breed of masochist.

title: Path of Exile

notes:
It works. I haven't had any crashes or other bugs so far, 12 hours in.
        
I use the following launch options: "PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 PROTON_NO_D3D11=0 PROTON_NO_D3D10=0 %command% --waitforpreload --nologo --nodx9ex"
        
Performance is ok with mostly 60 FPS with vsync on, occasionally dips into the low fifties. However, there are severe stutter/frame drops when a new effect/sound/shader/something is shown for the first time in a level. Using the performance graphs shown when pressing F1 I see frame times in the hundreds of ms, sometimes over a second. This is tolerable during regular play since the monsters/enemies aren't that challanging (yet?) but I worry that bosses later in the game will be able to kill me before I get past the stutter fest.
        
I tried with PROTON_USE_WINED3D but it gave worse performance than D9VK and the dynamic resolution scaling kicked in and turned the graphics into a blurry mess.
        
The two "PROTON_NO_D3D.+=0" options are probably not needed but I tested and the game won't start if any (or both) of them are set to 1.
        
"--waitforpreload" and "--nodx9ex" are there to reduce stutter while playing. "--nodx9ex" is particularly important as the 100 ms frame times would occur much more frequently until I added that.
title: Path of Exile

notes: Works nearly perfectly. There are a few issues with it sometimes just closing on its own but cannot say this is protons fault as there are a lot of windows users with similar issues. If you get CreateShaderResourceViewEx, CreateBuffer exceptions then you need to reduce the quality of textures since your GPU is running out of VRAM or make sure you have enough spare disk space. My launch options are '%command --waitforpreload -gc 1'. The reason for gold and not platinum is because of the CreateShaderResourceViewEx exceptions that keep happening since my GPU only has 3GB of VRAM, I am not sure this is an issue with proton or path of exile though.
title: Path of Exile

notes: Launch Options:   PROTON_USE_WINED3D --waitforpreload        Performance has improved greatly over the last few days.  Switching from Vulkan to OpenGL with PROTON_USE_WINED3D decreased the impact of strongboxes and legion monoliths to fairly tolerable levels.  But the real saving grace seems to be PoE's --waitforpreload option.  I'm tempted to give gold but considering I'm seeing max framerates between 100-120 whereas on windows it's 250-280.  I wouldn't expect it to be playable with a system closer to recomended specs.

notes:
Tweak used: PROTON_USE_WINED3D

What works: so far (finnished 1st chapter with new character) everything.
title: Path of Exile

notes: Popped in the old RX 580 and installed mesa-aco-git from the AUR.  In regards to stuttering, the ACO Mesa drivers are giving me a much more fluid gameplay experience than I was getting previously with the considerably faster RTX 2080.
title: Path of Exile

notes:
Playable all the way through with occasional performance issues due to
- lack of native client!
- server related issues (the game does a lot of stuff on the server)
- mediocre optimization.   
About half of the problems (plus some others) also occur on Windows.
A proper native Linux client still would make things a lot more user friendly.
title: Path of Exile

notes: I've played for quite a lot and cleared most of end-game zones including "blighted" maps without any issues. I've also made few premium shop purchases without any problems. Game works just perfectly but it can still crash when there is a lot of objects on the screen like on blighted maps with bunch of necromancers in your team. However, I still rate it as Platinum as the same issue can be experienced by people playing on Windows, therefore making those crashes unrelated to Proton itself.
title: Path of Exile

notes:
This game runs fine if you use a build of DXVK which has the async pipecompiler (also known as the "poe hack"). Stock proton does not come with with patch, so you need to install a different proton build to get it.

Currently I am using Tk-Glitch's proton (https://github.com/Tk-Glitch/PKGBUILDS/releases) with the following launch options to enable async pipecompiler: PROTON_DXVK_ASYNC=1 %command%

I have also tested GloriousEggroll's proton 4.11 (https://github.com/GloriousEggroll/proton-ge-custom/releases). It works with no additional launch options. DO NOT use any version after 4.11 for poe. For some reason, he removed the essential patch to make poe work from later versions. If you want to build one of the later versions for yourself, make sure you add the patch (https://github.com/jomihaka/dxvk-poe-hack) back in.
title: Path of Exile
